12.headline: Week 12 Recap: Pooh Pounces While the Coyotes Howl
12.intro: Week 12 delivered a pair of lopsided beatdowns at the top of the standings and a couple of grind-it-out category battles below them. The contenders separated themselves while the cellar grew a little darker.

matchups.narrative: The Pirates rode elite pitching to a comfortable 9-3 win, taking nine of twelve categories. David Glancy's staff posted a 3.64 ERA and a sparkling 1.088 WHIP, while the offense hit .302 with 52 runs to bury Chicago across the board. The Orphans claimed SB, QS, and HD, but were outclassed everywhere it mattered. Patrick Booth's club drops to 60-66 with the loss.
matchups.day_by_day: Chicago jumped out with a 6-run, 22-strikeout opener on 06-15, but cratered on 06-18 with a brutal -11 run swing. Pittsburgh built steadily, then sealed it with a 7-run, 3-homer eruption on 06-20 before both teams limped through the final day.
matchups.standout_players: Joey Cantillo was masterful for the Pirates with 8.0 IP, 9 K, and a 1.13 ERA, backed by Bryan Reynolds (2/5, HR, 3 RBI, 2 R). Bryce Harper led the Orphans with a 3/4, HR, RBI day, and MacKenzie Gore fanned 10 over 7 innings in a losing effort.

matchups.narrative: Phoenix steamrolled All Betts Are Off 10-2, dominating ten categories behind a monster 7 quality starts and 83 strikeouts. The Coyotes' .894 OPS and 17 home runs simply overwhelmed Andrew Powell's lineup. All Betts salvaged R and SB, but that was the extent of the resistance. Powell's squad falls to a league-worst 26-99.
matchups.day_by_day: Phoenix grabbed the early edge with a 4-run, 2-homer 06-15, while All Betts kept clawing back in runs throughout. The pitching gap proved decisive — Phoenix piled up 15 strikeouts on 06-19 alone, and the homer category was never close.
matchups.standout_players: Jac Caglianone went off for Phoenix with 2 HR, 3 RBI, and 3 R, while Cam Schlittler struck out 13 over 6 scoreless. Ivan Herrera (3/5, HR, 3 RBI, 3 R) and William Contreras (4/5, HR, 3 RBI) gave All Betts some pop, and Paul Skenes turned in a quality 6 IP, 8 K, 3.00 ERA.

matchups.narrative: Kevin Glancy's Pooh's On First put on a clinic, sweeping ten of eleven contested categories in an 11-1 rout — and the lone loss was simply a strategic punt on saves. The pitching was suffocating at a 2.96 ERA and 1.14 WHIP across nine quality starts, while the bats hit .270 with a .832 OPS. It was the most complete performance of the week, and it pushed Pooh's into the weekly roto crown. A statement showing from a team built to contend.
matchups.day_by_day: Lets Go Bucs actually opened strong with 6 runs and 3 homers on 06-15, but Pooh's answered with relentless quality starts and never let the Bucs back into the category fight. By midweek the rotation had quietly locked down ERA, WHIP, and QS, and the result was academic.
matchups.standout_players: Bobby Witt Jr (2/2, HR, 2 RBI, 2 R) and Pete Crow-Armstrong (2/4, HR, 2 RBI) led a balanced attack, while Nolan McLean fired 7 scoreless with 9 K. Dominic Canzone (HR, 2 RBI) and Parker Messick (6 IP, 9 K) were the bright spots for Sherri Glancy's Bucs.

matchups.narrative: The Big Gamblino took care of business 9-3, winning nine categories — as the league leader presumably should. Ted Zhang's bats clubbed a week-best 20 home runs and 53 RBI, and the team led the league in both, which is the kind of thing that happens when you've got Schwarber and friends. That said, the 4.85 ERA and 1.365 WHIP were hardly intimidating, and the Piglets did manage to snag SB, QS, and HD. A win is a win, even if the ratios weren't pretty.
matchups.day_by_day: The Piglets actually outscored Gamblino 9-6 with 5 homers on opening day 06-15, but couldn't sustain it. Gamblino's 06-20 power surge — 4 runs and 5 home runs — flipped the momentum for good as Pittsburgh's offense went quiet down the stretch.
matchups.standout_players: JJ Wetherholt powered Gamblino with a 3/5, 2 HR, 3 RBI, 3 R line, joined by Kazuma Okamoto and Kyle Schwarber (each 1 HR, 3 RBI). Jesus Luzardo added 7 IP, 9 K, 2.57 ERA. Nick Kurtz (HR, 3 RBI) and Eduardo Rodriguez (7 IP, 1.29 ERA) led the Piglets.

matchups.narrative: Cronen-Zone edged Seattle 7-4 in the week's most competitive matchup, leaning on power and pristine ratios. Andrew Stephens' squad smacked 18 homers with a 2.68 ERA and 1.19 WHIP despite a feeble .210 average. Seattle countered with strikeouts (95, a league high), quality starts, and steals, but the power gap and bullpen edge tipped the scales. The two sides split a tie in holds.
matchups.day_by_day: Seattle stayed close through a wild week of swings, including a 7-run 06-17 that kept things tight. The decider came on 06-21, when Cronen-Zone exploded for 9 runs and 4 homers to close out a back-and-forth battle.
matchups.standout_players: Pete Alonso (2/3, HR, 3 RBI, 2 R) and TJ Rumfield (HR, 3 RBI) carried Cronen-Zone's power, with Ranger Suarez tossing 6.2 scoreless. Logan Gilbert was excellent for Seattle (6.1 IP, 8 K, 1.42 ERA), but the Dumpers' bats managed just 5 homers all week.

12.roto_section: Pooh's On First seized the weekly roto crown with 118.5 points, fueled by top marks in RBI, SB, AVG, K, QS, ERA, and WHIP. Phoenix (106.0) and Pittsburgh Pirates (105.5) battled close behind, while The Big Gamblino (101.0) led the power categories with 20 HR and 53 RBI. At the bottom, All Betts Are Off limped to 63.0 points, salvaging only steals.
12.standings_note: The Big Gamblino remains atop the season standings at 83-41-8, but Phoenix (78-47) and a surging Pooh's On First (74-50) are keeping the pressure on. The gap at the bottom is widening, with All Betts Are Off mired at 26-99.
